seoTitle: Austin ISD Calendar 2025-2026: Holidays, Breaks & PDF
seoDescription: Austin ISD 2025-2026 calendar with Aug 19 first day, May 28 last day, holidays, breaks, staff development days, PDF and ICS.

calendarFaqs.q: When does school start for Austin ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: The first day of school for Austin Independent School District in 2025-2026 is Tuesday, August 19, 2025. Austin ISD's mid-August start is later than many North Texas districts; families arriving from states that begin school in early September should verify the Austin ISD date before booking end-of-summer travel. Families should plan to be back in Austin by August 16 (Saturday) to allow a weekend to prepare — Austin-Bergstrom International Airport and I-35 are typically busy the weekend before school starts.

calendarFaqs.q: When is the last day of school for Austin ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: The last day of school for Austin Independent School District in 2025-2026 is Thursday, May 28, 2026. The school year covers 180 instructional days from August 19, 2025 through May 28, 2026. Summer programs and camps starting June 1 or later align with the Austin ISD end date without a gap. Good Friday (April 6) is a no-school day, creating a slightly longer spring break window; the last school day is May 28 unless makeup days are needed.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Spring Break for Austin ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: Spring Break for Austin Independent School District in 2025-2026 runs Monday, March 16 through Friday, March 20, 2026 — five days. Students return Monday, March 23, 2026. This window typically overlaps with South by Southwest (SXSW), which runs in mid-March: Austin-Bergstrom Airport and downtown hotels are at or near peak demand during this period. Families planning out-of-town travel during Spring Break should book flights and accommodations by January; March prices and rental car availability deteriorate quickly as SXSW and Spring Break converge.

calendarFaqs.q: What are the unique no-school holidays in Austin ISD 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: Austin ISD 2025-2026 includes several no-school days not found on most other Texas district calendars: September 23 (Rosh Hashanah / Campus Staff Development), October 20 (Diwali / Parent Conference Day), February 17 (Lunar New Year / Parent Conference Day), and March 31 (Dolores Huerta Day). These culturally inclusive no-school days reflect Austin ISD's explicitly multicultural calendar policy. Families coordinating with children in neighboring districts (Round Rock ISD, Pflugerville ISD, Leander ISD) should note that these dates are specific to Austin ISD — neighboring districts will have regular school on these days.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Good Friday and Easter break for Austin ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: Easter Sunday falls April 5, 2026, and Austin ISD observes the following Monday, April 6, as a Campus Staff Development Day — no school for students. This is an unusual arrangement: most districts observe Good Friday (the Friday before Easter) as the no-school day, but in 2025-2026 Austin ISD's no-school day falls on Easter Monday. Families planning Easter weekend travel should plan around April 4–6 (Saturday through Monday). Spring Break (March 16–20) ends two weeks before Easter; the April 6 no-school day is separate and does not extend Spring Break.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Thanksgiving Break for Austin ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: Thanksgiving Break for Austin ISD in 2025-2026 runs Monday, November 24 through Friday, November 28 — the full school week. Students return Monday, December 1, 2025. A District Staff Development Day falls on November 4, creating a mid-fall no-school day approximately three weeks before Thanksgiving. Winter Break runs December 22 through January 1, with students returning Monday, January 5, 2026; January 5 is a regular school day (January 2–4 are the winter holiday days). A Campus Staff Development Day on January 5 pushes the first full school day to January 6 at most campuses — check the specific year's calendar.

yearNumbers.label: Rosh Hashanah
yearNumbers.detail: September 22–23 are both no-school days: September 22 is a District Staff Development Day and September 23 is a Campus Staff Development Day that coincides with Rosh Hashanah (the Jewish New Year). Two consecutive no-school days in the third week of September is unusual — families who aren't expecting them often get caught without childcare. Austin ISD is one of the few large Texas districts to formally include Rosh Hashanah on its no-school calendar. Neighboring Round Rock ISD and Pflugerville ISD do not observe this day — families coordinating across districts need to plan separately.

yearNumbers.label: Diwali
yearNumbers.detail: Diwali / Parent Conference Day on October 20, 2025 creates a mid-fall no-school day for students. Diwali 2025 falls on October 20 — Austin ISD's explicit alignment of a school closure with the Hindu festival of lights reflects the district's large South Asian community, concentrated particularly in North Austin and the Domain-area tech corridor. A PLED Day falls October 10 and Indigenous Peoples' Day on October 13, making October the month with the most no-school days outside of major holiday breaks. Families with childcare arrangements calibrated to the federal holiday calendar should review October specifically.

yearNumbers.label: Dolores Huerta Day
yearNumbers.detail: March 31, 2026 is Dolores Huerta Day — a no-school day unique to Austin ISD honoring civil rights and labor organizer Dolores Huerta. It falls just over a week after Spring Break ends on March 20, creating a secondary no-school day in late March. No other major Texas ISD observes this day as an official school closure. Families coordinating childcare or activities with neighboring district families should note that Dolores Huerta Day is specific to Austin ISD: Round Rock, Pflugerville, and Leander ISD will have regular school on March 31.

yearNumbers.label: Lunar New Year
yearNumbers.detail: Lunar New Year (Chinese New Year — Year of the Snake) falls on February 17, 2026, and Austin ISD observes it as a Parent Conference Day — no school for students. This creates a two-consecutive-day no-school stretch: Presidents' Day on February 16 and Lunar New Year / Parent Conference on February 17. For Austin's large East and Southeast Asian community — many employed at Samsung Austin Semiconductor, UT engineering programs, and tech sector campuses — the alignment of Lunar New Year with the Presidents' Day weekend is a meaningful scheduling recognition. Austin ISD is one of the few US school districts of this size to formally observe the Lunar New Year as an official no-school day.

events.name: Lunar New Year / Parent Conference Day

calendarNotes:
Austin ISD begins the 2025–2026 school year on Tuesday, August 19, 2025. Texas Education Agency (TEA) requires 180 instructional days; Austin ISD's mid-August start places it among the later-starting major Texas districts. The first weeks of school coincide with Austin's peak summer heat: August temperatures regularly exceed 100°F, and University Interscholastic League (UIL) heat protocols restrict outdoor athletic practices when the heat index reaches specified thresholds. Families new to Texas schools often find the August back-to-school period significantly hotter than expected — early-morning practices and afternoon pickup routines are shaped around the heat during the first month.

The 2025–2026 calendar includes a notable cluster of culturally inclusive no-school days that distinguish Austin ISD from neighboring Texas districts. September 22 and 23 are consecutive no-school days: the District Staff Development Day on September 22 and a Campus Staff Development Day on September 23 that coincides with Rosh Hashanah. Diwali (October 20) is observed as a Parent Conference Day — no school for students. Lunar New Year (February 17, 2026 — the first day of the Year of the Snake) is paired with a Parent Conference Day the day after Presidents' Day. March 31 is Dolores Huerta Day, a district-specific school closure honoring labor and civil rights organizer Dolores Huerta, unique to Austin ISD among major Texas districts. Families coordinating with children in neighboring Round Rock ISD, Pflugerville ISD, or Leander ISD should note these dates specifically — neighboring districts have regular school on all of them.

Spring Break 2025–2026 runs March 16–20. This window almost always overlaps with South by Southwest (SXSW), which typically runs in mid-March. When Spring Break and SXSW align, Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (AUS) departure traffic and downtown hotel occupancy peak simultaneously. Families planning out-of-town Spring Break travel should book flights, hotels, and rental cars by January — March pricing at AUS deteriorates rapidly as SXSW and Spring Break bookings accumulate. Dolores Huerta Day (March 31) falls about ten days after Spring Break ends, offering a secondary long-weekend opportunity for families who didn't travel during Spring Break.

Good Friday timing in 2025–2026 is unusual: Easter Sunday falls April 5, and Austin ISD observes April 6 (Easter Monday) as a Campus Staff Development Day rather than the traditional Good Friday the week before. Families accustomed to Good Friday school closures should verify the specific no-school date, which is April 6 rather than April 4 in this school year. The spring semester's remaining calendar after Good Friday runs through May 28, with Memorial Day (May 25) as the final holiday before year-end.

The school year closes Thursday, May 28, 2026. Summer programs and childcare starting June 1 accommodate the Austin ISD end date with a three-day gap. High school graduation ceremonies are typically held in late May or early June, with individual high school dates announced by spring. Families booking early June camps or travel should confirm the Austin ISD last day before committing, as the district occasionally adjusts the end date for weather-related makeup days. Post-school-year sailings, summer camp registrations, and family travel from AUS concentrate in the first week of June — popular departure dates and summer camp openings fill early.
